body:
This is part of the Codex component behavior and test coverage sweep. It fixes Field/Form behavior bugs found during review and adds focused test and adjacent sweep coverage for the affected paths.

## Changes

- Fix Field control name registration and Form values when the name lives on `Field.Control`.
- Fix stale validation values and stale async validation results.
- Apply `validationDebounceTime` consistently to field-aware controls.
- Preserve user `aria-describedby` values when Field adds description or error ids.
- Keep Field-provided `aria-invalid` authoritative when validation marks a control invalid.
- Fix controlled `dirty` required validation and `Form.errors` arrays.
- Reflect `Field.Item disabled` state on the item itself.
- Add test coverage for the failing and adjacent field-aware control paths.

## Original findings addressed

- [P2] `Field.Control name` is ignored by the Form registry. `Field.Root` documents its `name` as taking precedence over `Field.Control`'s `name`, but registration stores only the root name, so `<Field.Root><Field.Control name="email" /></Field.Root>` is omitted from `onFormSubmit` values and `validate(_, formValues)`.
- [P2] `actionsRef.validate()` can validate a stale value for custom validators. The imperative action calls `validation.commit(validityData.value)`, but normal typing only updates dirty/filled until a validation commit happens, so custom `validate(value)` can receive `null` or the previous committed value. Existing coverage only checks native `required`.
- [P2] Async validation has no stale-result guard. Each `commit` awaits `validate` and then unconditionally writes validity state, so a slower validation for an older value can overwrite the newer result. The async test only covers one validation pass.
- [P2] `validationDebounceTime` only works for `Field.Control`. Field-aware controls such as Select, RadioGroup, Checkbox, NumberField, Combobox, Slider, and Switch call `validation.commit()` directly when validating on change, bypassing the debounce path. Existing debounce coverage uses only `Field.Control`.
- [P2] User `aria-describedby` props can drop Field-provided description/error IDs. `Field.Control` merges validation props before `elementProps`, and `mergeProps` overwrites plain props, so consumer `aria-describedby` replaces Field's generated IDs instead of concatenating them. Tests only cover the automatic case.
- [P2] Controlled `dirty` does not affect required-field validation. `dirty={true}` updates public state, but `setDirty` returns before updating `markedDirtyRef`, so `valueMissing` can still be suppressed as if the field were pristine. The dirty prop test only checks style hooks.
- [P2] `Form.errors` arrays are typed but render as raw adjacent strings. Client validation arrays become a `<ul>`, while `Form errors={{ field: ['a', 'b'] }}` flows directly into `children`; tests only cover string server errors.
- [P3] `Field.Item disabled` is not reflected in `Field.Item` state or data attributes. It computes item-level disabled and passes it to wrapped controls, but `useRenderElement` receives the unmodified root state, so render props and `data-disabled` are wrong for the item itself.
title: [field] Fix form validation bugs
